title: Werauhia dressleri
intro: Werauhia dressleri, also known as Dressler's Werauhia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia dressleri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been isolated and studied for their medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Werauhia dressleri is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.

It is native to Colombia and has been identified as part of the genus Werauhia, which was previously known as Pitcairnia.
